commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=327d8ee33dcef75fed14257f0ad9567565db7eca)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes the module directive in `go.mod` from `go 1.26.3` to `go 1.26`.

Reproducibility: yes. extracting the PR head to scratch and running `GOTOOLCHAIN=go1.26.3 GOWORK=off go list -mod=readonly ./...` shows the proposed module metadata is incomplete because Go wants to update `go.mod`. The target checkout itself remains clean.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a contributor or maintainer update to the Go directive plus real install proof; missing contributor proof makes this a human-review path rather than a ClawSweeper repair job.

target.title: Fix invalid Go version directive in go.mod
target.body:
This PR fixes an invalid go directive in go.mod that prevents users from installing graincrawl with go install.

### Problem

The module currently declares:

`go 1.26.3`

But the go directive only accepts major.minor format, so patch versions are rejected by the Go toolchain.

This causes installs like the following to fail:

`go install github.com/openclaw/graincrawl/cmd/graincrawl@latest`

with:

`invalid go version '1.26.3': must match format 1.23`

### Fix

Changed:

`go 1.26.3`

to:

`go 1.26`

If patch-level toolchain pinning is desired, that should be represented with a separate toolchain directive instead.

### Result

go install github.com/openclaw/graincrawl/cmd/graincrawl@latest should work again for users with a compatible Go toolchain.
target.author: ejadib

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
